title: Session Manifest
description: Manifest handoff from briefing (or noted, for ad hoc) to noted for one meeting instance. Authoritative shape is the master plan §8. Once written, a manifest must not be modified; runtime state lives in separate files.

schema_version.description: Major.minor version of this manifest payload. Readers pinned to major 2 accept any 2.x payload; forward-tolerate minor (unknown fields ignored). Enforced as a major-2 pattern rather than a const so a 2.0 reader does not reject a 2.1 payload at schema-validation time (see `contracts/versioning-policy.md`).

session_id.description: Unique session identifier. Used as the session directory name.

created_at.description: Time the manifest was written. ISO-8601 with explicit timezone offset.

event_id.description: Calendar event identifier. Null only for ad hoc sessions (§20.1).

series_id.description: Matching briefing series, if any. Absent for ad hoc sessions.

start_time.description: Scheduled meeting start. ISO-8601 with explicit timezone offset.

scheduled_end_time.description: Scheduled meeting end. ISO-8601 with offset. Null only for ad hoc sessions (§20.1); end-of-meeting popup logic is suppressed in that case.

location_type.description: Optional briefing-owned routing label for multi-Mac setups, e.g. office or home. This is distinct from the free-form calendar location string and is not interpreted by noted.

type.description: Meeting capture mode. in_person captures the room microphone only. online and hybrid capture local microphone plus system audio.

names_are_hints_only.description: Locked to true. Participant names are soft hints only (guardrail 7; master plan §16.4). Producers must emit true; readers must refuse anything else.

max_single_extension_minutes.description: Reserved in v1.0 (master plan §8.3) for future extension policy (§27.12). Writers may populate; noted v1 ignores.

manifest_path.description: Absolute path to the pre-prepared next manifest. briefing writes this in advance; noted switch-next invokes noted start on it directly. Expected to be present when exists=true; absence is tolerated (switch-next will then return exit 3 or 8).

note_path.description: Target Obsidian note path. This is the only path outside session_dir that components are permitted to write (§11.4).

asr_backend.description: On-device Swift ASR backend. Locked for v1 per §15.2 and §27.9. Python backends (faster-whisper, whisperx) are not permitted.

speaker_count_hint.description: Advisory only. May be derived by briefing from attendees_expected or participant_names per §8.5.

language.description: BCP-47 language tag (e.g. en-AU). If absent, noted uses its configured default.

completion_callback.description: Reserved for a future per-session completion callback. Must be null in v1.0. See §8.3 and §27.6: completion handoff is performed by noted invoking `briefing session-ingest <session-dir>` directly.
